 {"id":"beads_rust-dxr9","title":"Track: replace external diff dependency with pure-Rust diff","description":"SUBTASK 7.2 (DEFERRED FEATURE): src/cli/commands/history.rs:443 contains:
  "Let us shell out to diff -u for now as it is standard on linux/mac."

CURRENT BEHAVIOR: The br history diff command shells out to the system diff -u binary via std::process::Command::new("diff"). This works on Linux and macOS but fails on Windows without WSL. It also contradicts the projects self-contained philosophy (no external tool dependencies).

DESIRED BEHAVIOR: Use a pure-Rust diff library to compute unified diffs internally. Candidates:
- similar crate (most popular, supports unified diff format)
- imara-diff crate (fast, used by gitoxide)
- dissimilar crate (by dtolnay, simpler API)

IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS:
- The diff output is displayed to the user in terminal, so it needs to produce unified diff format for familiarity
- The similar crate has TextDiff::from_lines().unified_diff() which produces exactly the right format
- Adding a dependency increases compile time and binary size — evaluate if the diff feature is used enough to justify it
- The current code already handles exit code interpretation (DiffCommandStatus enum) which would be simplified with an internal implementation
- Consider making this a feature flag to keep the binary small for users who do not use history diff

PRIORITY: P4/backlog because the history diff command is a low-frequency diagnostic tool, and most users are on Linux/macOS where diff -u works fine.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:28.620081349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:49:32.600208754Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:49:32.599857477Z","close_reason":"Replaced history diff's external diff subprocess with similar-based in-process unified diff rendering and JSON status classification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":245,"issue_id":"beads_rust-dxr9","author":"ubuntu","text":"DEPENDENCY EVALUATION: The similar crate adds ~50KB to binary size (acceptable for a CLI tool). It is well-maintained (by mitsuhiko) and already used by cargo, insta, and other Rust ecosystem tools. If binary size is a concern, feature-gate it behind a history-diff feature flag.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:56Z"}]}

-{"id":"beads_rust-e099","title":"[P3] Replace unreachable!() in src/cli/commands/show.rs:140","description":"Context: mock-code-finder audit found production unreachable!() at src/cli/commands/show.rs:140 in the routed show command's structured-output branch. The preceding matches! guard currently makes Text/Csv impossible, but if output-format routing changes this becomes a CLI panic. Proposed fix: refactor the branch into a single total match over OutputFormat or return a structured validation/internal error for impossible formats; add routed show coverage for JSON, TOON, and text/csv paths so the branch cannot regress.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:13:25.538292796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:41:18.310897022Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-eclx","title":"Epic: Sync Safety & JSONL Integrity","description":"Context:
- Plan mandates strict sync safety invariants (no git ops, .beads-only paths, atomic export, conflict detection).
- We need a cohesive epic to track hardening + tests.

Scope:
- JSONL export/import safety, path allowlist, preflight, history backups, logs.
- Unit, integration, and e2e coverage for invariants.

Out of scope:
- New sync features beyond plan (e.g., git automation).

Acceptance:
- Each child task under this epic completes its safety invariants and tests.
- br sync remains non-invasive and auditably safe.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:44:54.712027870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:08:01.755038345Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:08:01.755019570Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete: show field enhancements (beads_rust-1pt5), exit codes (beads_rust-33eh), JSONL import field preservation (beads_rust-bncj), sync preflight guardrails (beads_rust-1quj). Sync module hardened with JSON validation, prefix mismatch guard, and 86+ tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["safety","sync","tests"]}

Concrete target: extend the real-dataset write benchmark harness so it measures the missing local `update` and `close` workloads instead of only `create`.","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:41:14Z"},{"id":236,"issue_id":"beads_rust-nqtm","author":"codex","text":"Implemented the local write benchmark expansion in `tests/bench_real_datasets.rs`: the harness now captures created issue IDs from JSON output, then benchmarks the previously missing `update` and `close` workloads on isolated real-dataset workspace copies, with aggregate totals for create/update/close. Verification: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test --test bench_real_datasets -- --nocapture` all passed. The ignored `benchmark_beads_rust_only` smoke was runnable but skipped the true end-to-end path because `bd` is not installed in this environment.","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:51:32Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-nye2","title":"[P2] Fuzz: no coverage for search_issues free-text query input","description":"Gap: SqliteStorage::search_issues at src/storage/sqlite.rs:2413 accepts a free-form &str query from CLI args (br list --search <query>). This is an untrusted input surface with no fuzz coverage. While the query is used as a parameterized SQL bind value (not interpolated), the search logic includes string manipulation for FTS matching, LIKE pattern construction, and result filtering. Unusual inputs (NUL bytes, very long strings, Unicode edge cases, SQL-like syntax, empty strings) could trigger panics, logic errors, or unexpected query behavior. Related uncovered surfaces include add_comment (src/storage/sqlite.rs:5092, user-supplied text) and parse_date_reference (src/cli/commands/changelog.rs:336, user date strings). Target fuzz harness: new search_query fuzz target. Invariant to assert: (1) search_issues(&str) never panics on arbitrary input; (2) results are always a subset of stored issues (no phantom results); (3) error messages are non-empty on failure. Implementation: seed a SqliteStorage with known issues, then fuzz the search query string and verify results are valid issue IDs from the seeded set.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:04.982568315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:29:35.154296787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":321,"issue_id":"beads_rust-nye2","author":"cod_2","text":"degraded-coordination: files: fuzz/Cargo.toml, fuzz/fuzz_targets/search_issues.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:29:35Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-nye2","title":"[P2] Fuzz: no coverage for search_issues free-text query input","description":"Gap: SqliteStorage::search_issues at src/storage/sqlite.rs:2413 accepts a free-form &str query from CLI args (br list --search <query>). This is an untrusted input surface with no fuzz coverage. While the query is used as a parameterized SQL bind value (not interpolated), the search logic includes string manipulation for FTS matching, LIKE pattern construction, and result filtering. Unusual inputs (NUL bytes, very long strings, Unicode edge cases, SQL-like syntax, empty strings) could trigger panics, logic errors, or unexpected query behavior. Related uncovered surfaces include add_comment (src/storage/sqlite.rs:5092, user-supplied text) and parse_date_reference (src/cli/commands/changelog.rs:336, user date strings). Target fuzz harness: new search_query fuzz target. Invariant to assert: (1) search_issues(&str) never panics on arbitrary input; (2) results are always a subset of stored issues (no phantom results); (3) error messages are non-empty on failure. Implementation: seed a SqliteStorage with known issues, then fuzz the search query string and verify results are valid issue IDs from the seeded set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:04.982568315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:38.919580178Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:38.919264067Z","close_reason":"Added search_issues libFuzzer target that feeds arbitrary byte strings through SqliteStorage::search_issues, validates returned issues with IssueValidator, checks results are seeded issues, and verified with cargo fuzz check plus a 2000-run smoke session via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":321,"issue_id":"beads_rust-nye2","author":"cod_2","text":"degraded-coordination: files: fuzz/Cargo.toml, fuzz/fuzz_targets/search_issues.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:29:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-nz0","title":"ID Resolution & Prefix Matching","description":"# ID Resolution & Prefix Matching

## Purpose
Implement classic partial ID resolution and prefix validation used by show/update/close/dep/etc.

## Partial ID Resolution
Order:
1. Exact ID match (`SearchIssues` by IDs).
2. Normalize: if missing prefix, prepend `issue_prefix-` and retry exact match.
3. Substring match on hash portion across all prefixes.
4. Ambiguity => error with candidate list.

## Prefix Validation
- Explicit IDs must match `issue_prefix` or `allowed_prefixes` unless `--force`.
- Trailing hyphen in prefix input is accepted; stored prefix has **no** trailing hyphen.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Matches bd resolution order and ambiguity handling.
- Hierarchical IDs (`bd-abc.1`) supported.

## Tests
- Exact, prefix, hash-only, substring matching.
- Ambiguous match error lists candidates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:17:23.123240361Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T14:01:13.998727366Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T14:01:13.998727366Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete in src/util/id.rs. Added IdResolver, ResolverConfig, MatchType, ResolvedId, find_matching_ids, resolve_id with comprehensive tests. Note: codebase has unrelated compilation issues from other agents WIP that block cargo test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-nzfl","title":"MCP tools reject malformed field value types","description":"Round-2 review found MCP tool handlers silently ignore malformed JSON values for schema-declared fields. update_issue treats non-string title/status/priority/type keys as requested field updates while parse_update_fields drops them, producing misleading no-op behavior instead of InvalidParams. create_issue and label/comment side effects similarly drop malformed optional values. Validate declared MCP input field types before mutation and add focused unit coverage.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:41:05.363730179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:49:57.610013699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:49:57.609700392Z","close_reason":"Fixed MCP malformed JSON handling by rejecting wrong-type required/optional fields, strict label array entries, and the date-only timestamp panic surface; added focused MCP unit tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-o1az","title":"Scenario tagging + selection filters","description":"Allow users to run subsets of scenarios by tag (quick, full, conformance, bench, sync, error, stress).

Scope
- Tag each scenario in the DSL.
- Provide CLI/env filters to select tags or exclude heavy tests.
- Log selected tags in summary.json for auditability.

Acceptance
- Users can run targeted subsets without editing code; logs show exact selection criteria.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:56:02.091666766Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:38:44.138997707Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:38:44.138997707Z","close_reason":"ScenarioFilter already implemented with tag include/exclude, env vars (HARNESS_TAGS, HARNESS_EXCLUDE_TAGS, HARNESS_TAG_MATCH), to_json() for summary logging, integrated with ScenarioRunner","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-o1az","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-o1az","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-enep","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-o1az","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
